title: Uncaught JSON parse errors in show/done commands expose raw stack traces
description: The tapShow, tapDone, actShow, meoShow, specShow, and mistakeShow command handlers called fs.readJsonSync() without try/catch. A malformed or adversarially crafted artifact JSON file (e.g. truncated file, embedded null bytes, deeply nested structure) would cause an unhandled SyntaxError or TypeError to surface as a raw Node.js stack trace, potentially revealing internal file paths, dependency versions, and call stack structure to an attacker who can observe CLI output.
impact: Information disclosure of internal call stack. In adversarial scenarios (shared CI logs, piped output), stack traces can reveal internal module paths and dependency versions useful for crafting further attacks.
mitigation: All 6 affected show/done handlers wrapped in try/catch. On parse failure, a user-friendly error message is shown and the process exits with code 1. No stack trace is exposed. Mitigated in v2.2.0.
